channel.title: # 🇱🇰 Sri Lanka This Week
channel.description: Generated by gpt-5 from 98 English News Articles published between 2026-04-08 and 2026-04-14.

item.title: Sri Lanka and the IMF reached a staff-level agreement on the fifth and sixth reviews of the four-year Extended Fund Facility.
item.description: The deal unlocked roughly $700m, lifting total disbursements to about $2.4bn 💰, pending consideration by the lender’s Executive Board. Authorities completed the combined review despite recent external shocks, keeping the reform programme on track with tighter public finances and rebuilt reserves.

item.title: The Colombo Stock Exchange rallied on 8 April and 10 April, lifting benchmark indices and turnover.
item.description: On the first session, the main gauge rose 885.14 points to 21,917.60 with Rs. 6.61bn in trades, while the blue‑chip index climbed 257.68 points to 6,090.50. Two days later, gains of 284 points for the broad market and Rs. 5bn in activity signalled improved risk appetite as crossings in large caps and banks supported volumes 📈.

item.title: Parliament scrutinised a LKR 13.2 billion fraud disclosed at National Development Bank.
item.description: The legislature recorded that regulators ordered a cash‑dividend freeze, curbs on discretionary spending, and the appointment of an independent forensic auditor. Lawmakers noted that losses reported days earlier had escalated sharply, prompting questions about supervision and internal controls at the listed lender.

item.title: Colombo Dockyard announced new board appointments including a non-executive chairman and two nominee directors.
item.description: The changes, effective 7 April 2026, followed the acquisition of a 51% stake by India’s Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders Ltd., which also confirmed the incumbent managing director as chief executive. Additional nominees included senior figures from local conglomerates and investment groups, reinforcing governance at the shipbuilder.

item.title: The Government launched a Rs. 2.16 billion upgrade of Colombo Fort Railway Station under a project co-financed by the Asian Development Bank.
item.description: Planned works over 15 months included automated ticketing, lifts and overhead bridges for accessibility, new safety‑oriented buildings, and higher‑standard sanitation 🔧. The initiative formed part of a broader efficiency drive in urban transport, with senior officials present at the kickoff ceremony.

item.title: Train services on the Northern Line resumed after cyclone damage was repaired with Indian grant support.
item.description: The restoration formed part of a $5m component within a wider $450m package, and was completed ahead of the Sinhala and Tamil New Year 🚆. Reopening the corridor reconnected communities and freight across affected districts following repairs to track and related infrastructure.

item.title: Commercial Bank partnered with NCG Express to roll out fully digital bus ticketing on private routes.
item.description: Conductors used handheld devices to accept debit and credit cards, QR‑based payments, and mobile wallets, with instant settlement through the acquiring network 🚌. Initial deployment began on selected services, with plans to scale across the fleet to reduce cash handling and improve transparency.

item.title: Vidullanka acquired 100% of Bhoruka Power Lanka, adding the Gurugoda mini hydro plant to its portfolio.
item.description: The 4.5 MW facility in Aranayake became the company’s 12th hydro asset, expanding a base that also included new ground‑mounted solar plants and awarded wind and storage projects ⚡. Recent activity featured a 30% stake in a wind platform, an engineering contract completion in Guyana, and lowest‑bid status for a mini‑hydro in Fiji.

item.title: The Motor Traffic Commissioner General was further remanded until 20 April in a vehicle registration case.
item.description: The suspect, arrested on 27 March by the Criminal Investigation Department, faced allegations of aiding and abetting the fraudulent registration of a cab by altering its chassis number. A magistrate extended custody after the production of the detainee in court pending continued investigation.

item.title: A Parliamentary Select Committee examined legal options to restart Provincial Council elections stalled since the 2017 reforms.
item.description: Officials informed members that the delimitation process required for the mixed system had not been finalised, leaving no operative framework to poll. The Attorney General’s Department stated that repealing the amendment and re‑enacting the prior law was feasible, and the committee decided to summon the Delimitation Commission for further evidence ⚖️.
